name: Birth of the Rio Guadalaviar — circular through the Montes Universales from Griegos
name_original: Nacimiento del Río Guadalaviar — circular por los Montes Universales desde Griegos
region_intro: Mountain biking around Griegos offers routes through the Montes Universales, characterized by varied terrain including forested areas and river valleys. The region features a mix of moderate and difficult mountain bike trails, providing options for experienced riders. Elevations in the area contribute to challenging climbs and descents, typical of mountain environments. The landscape is shaped by the Rio Guadalaviar, adding natural features to the routes. Best mountain bike trails around Griegos The most popular mountain bike trail is Birth of the Rio Guadalaviar — circular through the Montes Universales from Griegos, a 30.1 miles (48.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 9 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the headwaters of the Rio Guadalaviar within the Montes Universales. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Montañas Vacías Loop, a difficult 47.9 miles (77.0 km) path. This challenging loop offers extensive riding through remote mountain landscapes. Local mountain bikers also love the Griegos loop from Griegos, a 29.8 miles (48.0 km) trail leading through the local mountain terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 53 minutes. Mountain biking around Griegos is defined by mountain passes, river valleys, and dense woodlands. The network offers options for different ability levels, with a focus on moderate and difficult routes. The routes in Griegos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Griegos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What level of difficulty can I expect from mountain bike trails in Griegos?
A: The mountain bike trails around Griegos are primarily suited for experienced riders, featuring a mix of moderate and difficult routes. There are no easy trails available in this area, so riders should be prepared for challenging climbs and descents through varied terrain.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Griegos?
A: No, the mountain bike trails in Griegos are not recommended for beginners. All available routes are classified as either moderate or difficult, requiring a good level of fitness and technical skill.

Q: What is the typical length and duration of mountain bike tours in Griegos?
A: Mountain bike tours in Griegos vary in length and duration. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004953584/birth-of-the-rio-guadalaviar-circular-through-the-montes-universales-from-griegos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Birth of the Rio Guadalaviar — circular through the Montes Universales from Griegos&lt;/a&gt; is about 30.1 miles (48.4 km) and takes around 4 hours 9 minutes. More challenging routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004921016/montanas-vacias-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montañas Vacías Loop&lt;/a&gt; extend to 47.9 miles (77.0 km) and can take over 6 hours.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes available in Griegos?
A: Yes, Griegos offers several excellent circular mountain bike routes. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004953584/birth-of-the-rio-guadalaviar-circular-through-the-montes-universales-from-griegos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Birth of the Rio Guadalaviar — circular through the Montes Universales from Griegos&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31430859" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Griegos loop from Griegos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Where are the main starting points or trailheads for mountain biking in Griegos?
A: Many mountain bike tours start directly from Griegos. Other routes may begin from nearby villages such as Villar del Cobo or Guadalaviar, offering different perspectives of the Montes Universales.

Q: What interesting places can I see while mountain biking around Griegos?
A: While mountain biking around Griegos, you can explore various points of interest. Notable attractions include the historic settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2530405" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orihuela del Tremedal&lt;/a&gt;, the charming village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5376107" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Griegos&lt;/a&gt; itself, and the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4542170" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molino de las Pisadas&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3924939" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Portera Refuge and Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; or the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3620455" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Alta Summit&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any refuges or huts along the mountain bike trails in Griegos?
A: Yes, the area around Griegos features several refuges and huts that can provide shelter or a resting point. These include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3924939" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Portera Refuge and Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4040458" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forest House Refuge&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3497950" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Collado Refuge&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3925554" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Lorenzo Hermitage and Shelter&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: When is the best time of year for mountain biking in Griegos?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Griegos is typically from late spring through early autumn (May to October). During these months, the weather is generally more stable and pleasant, and the trails are less likely to be affected by snow or heavy rain, which can occur in the colder seasons due to the region's elevation.

Q: Can I go mountain biking in Griegos during winter?
A: Mountain biking in Griegos during winter can be challenging due to the region's mountainous terrain and potential for snow and ice. While some trails might be accessible, conditions can be unpredictable, and many routes may be covered in snow, making them unsuitable for mountain biking. It's advisable to check local weather and trail conditions before planning a winter ride.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Griegos?
A: The mountain bike trails in Griegos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the challenging climbs and descents, and the remote mountain landscapes, particularly on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004921016/montanas-vacias-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montañas Vacías Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any less crowded mountain bike trails in Griegos?
A: While Griegos offers a network of trails, some of the longer and more difficult routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004921016/montanas-vacias-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montañas Vacías Loop&lt;/a&gt;, tend to be less trafficked due to their demanding nature. Exploring these more extensive routes can offer a more solitary experience amidst the remote mountain landscapes.
tips:
Griegos is a charming village located in the Albarracín mountain range. 
Griegos is also known for being one of the highest villages in Spain, at 1,601 meters above sea level. It's also a perfect place to enjoy outdoor activities such as horseback riding, hiking, and mountain biking.

The official source of water is located at Fuente de García, in the Montes Universales, within the municipality of Frías de Albarracín (Teruel). It is located at an altitude of approximately 1,590 meters, surrounded by pine forests and high mountain meadows.
Access is very easy and well signposted. Take the A-1704 highway, which connects Frías de Albarracín with Huélamo (Cuenca). From the highway, a paved detour of approximately 2 km takes you directly to the recreational area and the monument. There is free parking next to the monument.

This shelter serves as a resting place and even as a place to spend the night during hikes. The surrounding area features stone tables and benches where you can sit and eat while enjoying the surroundings.
